description_long: This object consists of six red cylindrical sticks bundled together with two wide greenish-blue bands. On top of the bundle sits a blue electronic circuit board featuring various components, including capacitors, resistors, and a prominent orange cylindrical element. Several wires, both red and white, are visible, some connecting the circuit board to the sticks and others protruding outward. The ends of the sticks are capped in a lighter color, suggesting a sealed or insulated design. The overall construction is compact and utilitarian, with the electronic board clearly intended to interact with the bundled cylinders. The visual cues strongly indicate a device designed for detonation, with the circuit board likely serving as the triggering mechanism.
description: A bundle of six red cylindrical sticks secured with greenish-blue bands, topped with a blue electronic circuit board and visible wiring.
description_view_0: The side view shows the six red sticks bundled together with two greenish-blue bands and the blue circuit board with electronic components on top.
description_view_1: The end-on view reveals the circular arrangement of the six sticks and the full width of the circuit board, with wires extending outward.
description_view_2: The opposite side view mirrors view 0, showing the bundled sticks, bands, and the circuit board, with more wiring visible.
description_view_3: The rear end-on view highlights the capped ends of the sticks, the arrangement of the wires, and the underside of the circuit board.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The device features a blue electronic circuit board with visible wiring and components, suggesting detonation could be triggered electronically, possibly via a timer or remote signal.
